items: The market entered with unresolved U.S.-Iran escalation risk after the prior day's oil spike.
AI/chip shares were attempting to stabilize after renewed selling.
Any diplomatic headline had unusually high authority because recent truce signals had repeatedly reversed.
scheduled_events: NATO summit remarks and U.S.-Iran developments

intraday_and_postclose_catalysts: President Trump said the interim Iran deal was 'over' and warned of additional strikes.
Oil and geopolitical risk increased while the Dow and S&P weakened.
Broadcom and selected battered chip shares rebounded, creating mixed index internals.
causal_narrative: Geopolitical headline risk overrode the attempted stabilization. The S&P 500 declined because renewed war escalation raised oil, inflation and duration uncertainty. Selected chip rebounds prevented a more uniform technology collapse, but the session's dominant causal force was the breakdown of the diplomatic narrative.
alternative_explanations: Options and dealer positioning may amplify or oppose the public-news impulse.
Index concentration can make SPY and SPX diverge from equal-weight breadth.
